title: lotodes canescens
intro: Lotodes canescens, also known as the 'woolly waterwort', is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the family Haloragaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lotodes canescens include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions and decoctions, which are often consumed as teas or used topically in the form of creams or ointments.
